source_paper_title: Specific Wasserstein Divergence Between Continuous Martingales
context:
Page 8, Remark 2.4 (end of remark)

We consider the scaling limit of (2.1) for our choice \(\alpha_1(\cdot) = \pm W_1(\cdot, P)^p\) as an interesting open question which we do not tackle here.
title: Scaling limit of dynamic risk-measure duality with Wasserstein-type one-step penalty
latex:
Let \(\Omega=C([0,1];\mathbb{R})\) be the canonical path space and let \(X_t(\omega)=\omega_t\). Fix \(p>0\) and a reference law \(P\in\mathcal{P}(\Omega)\) of a continuous martingale (e.g. a diffusion as in Assumption 2.7 of the paper). For each \(N\in\mathbb{N}\), let \((P)^N\in\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{R}^N)\) be the law of \((X_{1/N},\dots,X_{N/N})\) under \(P\), and define the time-consistent discrepancy \(D^{N,p}_W(Q\|\|P)\) on \(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{R}^N)\) by
\[
D^{1,p}_W(Q\|\|P)=W_1(Q,P)^p,\qquad
D^{N,p}_W(Q\|\|P)=D^{N-1,p}_W(Q_{1:N-1}\|\|P_{1:N-1})+\int W_1\big(Q^{x_{1:N-1}}_N,P^{x_{1:N-1}}_N\big)^p\,dQ_{1:N-1}(x_{1:N-1}).
\]
For a bounded continuous functional \(G\in C_b(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{R}))\), define on \(\mathbb{R}^N\) the functional \(F_N(x_1,\dots,x_N)=N\,G\big(\frac1N\sum_{i=1}^N\delta_{x_i}\big)\). Consider the robust pricing/dynamic risk-measure duality functional
\[
\Phi_N:=\inf_{H\ \mathrm{predictable}}\ \rho_N\big(F_N(X)-(H\cdot X)_N\big),
\]
where \(\rho_N\) is the dynamic risk measure generated by a one-step penalty \(\alpha_1(q)=\pm W_1(q, P_1)^p\) (with the associated multi-step penalty \(\alpha_N(Q)=\int \sum_{i=1}^N \alpha_1(Q_i^{x_{1:i-1}})\,dQ\)). Equivalently (formally, via minimax),
\[
\Phi_N=\sup\Big\{\int F_N\,dQ-\alpha_N(Q):\ Q\in\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{R}^N)\ \text{such that }(X_i)_{i=1}^N\text{ is a discrete-time martingale under }Q\Big\}.
\]
Determine the correct scaling \(c_N\) and characterize the limit
\[
\lim_{N\to\infty} c_N\,\Phi_N,
\]
including an explicit variational formula (e.g. in terms of continuous-time martingale laws \(Q\) on \(\Omega\) and a functional of the quadratic variation density), under suitable regularity assumptions on \(P\) and \(G\).
open_problem_quote: We consider the scaling limit of (2.1) for our choice \(\alpha_1(\cdot) = \pm W_1(\cdot, P)^p\) as an interesting open question which we do not tackle here.
subject_classification: Stochastic Control / Dynamic Risk Measures / Martingale Optimal Transport
